# Phase 9.2.1 Final Skepticism Archive Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, or deployment authority.

## Objective

Consolidate constitutional skepticism, restraint doctrine, blocker lineage, survivability evidence, unresolved-risk tracking, non-activation rationale, fail-closed evidence, and anti-escalation continuity into a permanent documentary archive.

## Scope

- Final skepticism archive doctrine.
- Constitutional evidence consolidation.
- Restraint-lineage preservation.
- Runtime skepticism archive systems as documentary structure.
- Unresolved-risk archive doctrine.
- Constitutional objection preservation.
- Fail-closed evidence continuity archive.
- Anti-activation institutional memory.
- Permanent restraint record doctrine.

## Required Safeguards

- No runtime activation exists.
- No provider execution exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No deployment authority exists.
- No execution-capable infrastructure exists.
- No skepticism-archive system creates runtime authority.
- No evidence-consolidation layer bypasses constitutional supremacy.
- No restraint-memory process enables hidden execution escalation.
- No archive-governance layer creates infrastructure activation authority.
- No skepticism-preservation framework weakens auditability.

## Step Improvement Notes

- Correct build order: follows restraint closure and tribunal preparation.
- Safe to include now: documentary archive doctrine and evidence consolidation standards.
- Future upgrade: tribunal quorum, evidence admissibility, and anti-bias safeguards.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, API integrations, autonomous governance, or execution systems.
